expect: Human signals override prior evidence in ≥3 cases — directional authority substitutes for epistemic authority
method: Systematic audit of all human-originated PHIL claims. For each, determine if evidence existed BEFORE the human signal (EVIDENCE-CREATED) or the signal was the sole cause (AUTHORITY-CREATED). Sample: PHIL-18, PHIL-25, PHIL-26, B20.

cases.human_text: how can there be something from nothing
cases.notes: L-491 created AFTER signal as response. Conceptual analogy, not measurement. S432 reclassified axiom/conceptual.

cases.human_text: swarm gather swarm for what is fair swarm
cases.notes: Unnamed for 476 sessions. fairness_audit.py built AFTER signal. First measurement (S497) showed 0.4/1.0.
Mathematical framework applied retroactively. DROPPED S520 (2/4 predictions falsified). 0 actionable improvements in 25 sessions.

cases.human_text: develop belief and dream for a swarm whose nodes are themselves swarms
cases.notes: Explicitly filed as theorized (n=0). 0 evidence after 60+ sessions. Structurally unfalsifiable at n=0.

actual: 4/4 human-originated PHIL claims are AUTHORITY-CREATED. Pattern: signal→claim→evidence, not observation→hypothesis→test. PHIL-13 is PARTIALLY FALSIFIED: challenge resolution is evidence-routed (OR=8.5x), but belief creation is authority-routed (4/4). The claim has two truth pathways — it only describes one.
diff: Expected ≥3, found 4/4. The authority-routing pattern is universal for human-signal-initiated claims, not occasional.
effect_size: 4/4 (100%) human-originated PHIL claims lack pre-signal evidence. Challenge resolution OR=8.5x confirms evidence pathway. The gap between creation-pathway (0% evidence-routed) and resolution-pathway (OR=8.5x evidence-routed) is the falsification.
external_grounding: Agenda-setting theory (McCombs & Shaw 1972): who controls what gets discussed controls conclusions. Kuhn paradigm theory: normal science occurs within authority-set paradigms. Lakatos: core commitments are protected by auxiliary belt.
conclusion: PHIL-13 PARTIALLY FALSIFIED. The claim conflates two distinct truth pathways. Recommend updating PHIL-13 to acknowledge dual-pathway structure: evidence routes challenge resolution; authority routes belief creation.
